item.title: Chile President Lays Out Security Agenda and Vandal Black List
item.description: Chile President José Antonio Kast said in his first State of the Nation address that he’ll roll out new public security measures in coming weeks, including one to create a black list of “vandals” who would lose the right to some social benefits.

item.title: HPE Sees Strong Growth Over Next 18 Months on Server Demand
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. gave an outlook for annual sales that topped estimates, citing massive growth in demand for the company’s servers and networking that it expects to continue through next year. The shares soared in extended trading.

item.title: GoPro Warns of Going-Concern Risk Amid AI-Fueled Memory Crunch
item.description: Surging memory costs are pushing action-camera maker GoPro Inc. to the brink. The company, founded by Nicholas Woodman, warned of risks to its ability to continue as a going concern and is seeking financing to avert a default, according to a latest filing.

item.title: BMO Hires TD’s van Arragon for Domestic Business Banking Role
item.description: Bank of Montreal hired Trevor van Arragon from rival Toronto-Dominion Bank for a newly created role leading BMO’s Canadian business banking division.

item.title: M&A Lawyer Pleads Not Guilty to Leading Insider-Trading Ring
item.description: A former mergers and acquisitions lawyer with top firms including Latham & Watkins and Goodwin Procter pleaded not guilty to charges that he led a massive insider trading ring that made tens of millions of dollars in illegal profits.

item.title: For Goldman’s Top Bankers, It’s All AI Data Centers All the Time
item.description: For leveraged finance practitioners, artificial intelligence is the only game in town — especially in the absence of more debt deals to finance mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: Shareholders Agree to Split Up Assets of Czech Utility Giant CEZ
item.description: Shareholders approved a proposal to split up the assets of the Czech Republic’s dominant power utility, a step toward a government plan to buy out minority investors and gain full control over the company’s power production.

item.title: White House Reviews SEC, CFTC Bid to Revisit Swaps Reporting
item.description: The White House is reviewing an early-stage move from the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission to revisit reporting requirements for both swaps and security-based swaps, as the two market regulators continue their effort to better coordinate.

item.title: Analysts Tell OPEC+ Hormuz Disruption to Last Through Year End
item.description: Oil industry experts have told OPEC+ that supply disruption caused by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz will persist to the end of the year, even if the waterway reopens promptly.

item.title: Apple Readies Feature to Split Bills by Taking Photo of a Receipt
item.description: Apple Inc. is planning a new service for the iPhone that will let users split bills for group dinners or other events, expanding its push into everyday financial tools and challenging third-party apps.

item.title: US Spot Petrochemical Prices Ease as Export Demand Starts to Lag
item.description: US spot petrochemical prices softened across the board last week as export demand for the plastics building blocks appears to weaken for the month of June.

item.title: Quant Fails to Get $1 Billion Source-Code Theft Charge Tossed
item.description: An ex-Headlands Technologies LLC quantitative trader must face a criminal charge that he stole source code that his former employer spent more than $1 billion to develop.

item.title: True Crime Podcaster Audiochuck Replaces CEO With SoulCycle Head
item.description: Evelyn Webster has been named the new chief executive officer of the company behind Crime Junkie

item.title: Torrid Rally in Rocket Stocks Falters as SpaceX IPO Draws Close
item.description: A seemingly boundless rally in space-related firms is showing further signs of weakness, with several stocks stumbling for a second day on Monday, as the public debut of Elon Musk’s SpaceX looms.

item.title: Bank of Canada Warns Against Overreacting to Techical Recession Indicator
item.description: Bank of Canada Senior Deputy Governor Carolyn Rogers cautioned against concluding the country is in a recession after recent data showed the economy contracted for a second consecutive quarter.

item.title: China’s SAIC to Make MG Cars at First European Factory in Spain
item.description: SAIC Motor Corp.’s MG unit has selected Spain for its first European car factory, a move that will help the Chinese automaker soften the blow of European Union tariffs.

item.title: Citadel Securities Seeks to Extend and Upsize $4 Billion Loan
item.description: Citadel Securities is seeking to reshape roughly $4 billion of existing debt after reporting record trading revenue, joining a flock of companies looking to reduce borrowing costs or extend maturities of existing credit.

item.title: InPost Expands France Footprint With New €500 Million Investment to Support Organic Growth
item.description: InPost SA is increasing its presence in France with an additional €500 million ($581 million) investment by 2030 to support organic growth, as Poland’s largest parcel locker operator expands across Europe.

item.title: Illinois Passes $56 Billion Budget as Bears Deal Stalled
item.description: Illinois legislators early Monday morning passed a nearly $56 billion budget for the fiscal year starting July 1, aiming to ease higher health-care and housing costs while bracing for potential federal funding cuts.

item.title: Salesforce Investment in Anthropic Is Valued at About $5 Billion
item.description: Salesforce Inc. has a stake in Anthropic PBC worth about $5 billion after repeatedly investing in the ascendant AI startup.

item.title: Anthropic Files Confidentially for IPO in Race With OpenAI
item.description: Anthropic PBC has confidentially submitted draft paperwork for a public listing, potentially leapfrogging longtime rival OpenAI in the race toward a Wall Street debut as soon as this fall.

item.title: Florida Sues OpenAI, Sam Altman Over Chatbot Safety Concerns
item.description: The state of Florida sued OpenAI and Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman, accusing the artificial intelligence company of ignoring safety warnings and releasing its ChatGPT product while knowing it was harmful to users.

item.title: Europe’s Junk Firms Look to Cut Loan Costs in Hot Market
item.description: Borrowers are rushing to Europe’s leveraged loan market at the start of the week to reprice debt, as they seek to capitalize on favorable credit conditions to cut their margins.

item.title: Fluence Shares Soar on Design for Nvidia AI Data Center Factory
item.description: Fluence Energy Inc. shares soared more than 40% after the US energy storage company said it collaborated with Siemens AG and Nvidia Corp. to develop a design for artificial intelligence data centers.

item.title: US Crude Refiners Are Pushing Run Rates to Maximum Levels
item.description: US oil refiners are running their plants harder than usual, with some even putting off maintenance, as strong profits and steady demand for fuels tempt the processors to run plants near maximum capacity.

item.title: CoreWeave-Tied Data Center’s Junk Bond Sale Seeks $850 Million
item.description: Elk Grove Village Property LLC is looking to raise $850 million from a junk-bond sale to help fund a data center tied to CoreWeave Inc., joining a wave of high-yield issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: FS KKR Is Selling $400 Million Bonds in Junk-Rated BDC Deal
item.description: A private credit fund jointly managed by Future Standard and KKR & Co. is looking to sell at least $400 million of junk bonds, according to people familiar with the matter, in a rare high-yield offering by a publicly traded credit fund.

item.title: Power Company ERock Seeks to Raise $642 Million in IPO
item.description: ERock Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $642 million from an initial public offering, joining a rush of listings ahead of SpaceX later this month.

item.title: Dollar Opens Week Boosted by AI Enthusiasm, Haven Demand
item.description: Traders are betting the US dollar will rise in the weeks ahead, supported by lingering uncertainty over US-Iran talks and an AI-fueled rally in US stocks.

item.title: IBM Shares Surge as Old Clip of Trump Praise Recirculates Online
item.description: A nearly six-month old video of Donald Trump praising International Business Machines Corp.’s CEO sent the shares surging almost 9% on Monday, adding more than $24 billion to the company’s stock-market value.
